methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Viridian Space is a pre-seed stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by Rostislav Spektor and Matthew Feldman, having raised $2M+ to date. The sector comp universe consists of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, though only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints.

Stage-progression analysis anchors on the $2M total raised as a proxy base value. Applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from pre-seed to the subsequent funding stage yields an implied valuation of $5M. This method is grounded in observed funding velocity within the stage, though it reflects trajectory rather than fundamental value creation. Peer-median analysis could not be applied: while 25 same-sector peers exist, zero met the filters for identical or adjacent stage with sufficient comparable data. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples was similarly inapplicable; the sector median P/S stands at 42.9x across the five public names, but Viridian's revenue is not disclosed and revenue-based multiples require disclosed financials.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $4M to $6M, centered at $5M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method and underscores material uncertainty: the absence of disclosed revenue, comparable pre-seed transactions in the narrow peer set, and early-stage profitability data all constrain precision. Key downside drivers include technical execution risk and competitive density in launch services; upside hinges on near-term technical validation and capital efficiency relative to sector benchmarks.
